name: Potential Data Exfiltration Via Curl
notes:
This rule detects the execution of the "curl" process with "upload" flags. Which might indicate potential data exfiltration

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Florian Roth
SIGMA Title: Suspicious Curl File Upload
SIGMA Description: Detects a suspicious curl process start the adds a file to a web request


Confidence: HIGH

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Success
when the event matches Command (custom) contains any of curl
when the event matches Command (custom) contains any of [ -F or --form or -T or --upload-file or -d or --data or --data-]
TD_list: APPLY Potential Data Exfiltration Via Curl on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success, Audit.Command Execution Success
AND when the event matches Command (custom) contains any of curl
AND when the event matches Command (custom) contains any of [ -F  or  --form or  -T  or  --upload-file  or  -d  or  --data  or  --data-]
RED_list: Event name: Potential Data Exfiltration Via Curl.EXE
Event description: Detects the execution of the "curl" process with "upload" flags. Which might indicate potential data exfiltration
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
